question: Based on the chart of rainfall (mm) over the recent two‑week period, which statement about the distribution of rainfall over time is most accurate?

options.A: Rainfall was generally higher in the latter part of the two‑week period than in the earlier part.
options.B: Rainfall steadily decreased from the start to the end of the two‑week period.
options.C: Rainfall totals were exactly the same on every day shown.
options.D: It is impossible to identify any differences in rainfall over the period.

explanation: The x‑axis order has been scrambled: the bars are not shown in true chronological sequence. If you reorder the days from Day 1 to Day 14 and sum the first and last 7 days, you see that the last segment actually has the greater total. Judging only from the apparent left‑to‑right pattern would give the wrong conclusion.
